headline: LIVESTOCK EXPLODES AS GRAIN COMPLEX CRATERS
subheadline: Hogs surge 8.6% overnight while soybeans collapse 3.8% in stunning reversal that flips feed cost math.
lead: The agricultural complex split violently overnight with livestock futures exploding higher while grains and oilseeds cratered. Lean hogs rocketed 8.6% to $93.50 — the biggest single-session move in over a year — while soybeans collapsed 3.8% to $11.55 as fund liquidation accelerated. The divergence creates a massive shift in feed cost dynamics just as spring planting decisions lock in.
teaser: Overnight chaos flips the feed cost equation as hogs explode and beans crater.

one_number.context: This is the biggest single-session hog rally in 14 months, driven by technical buying after funds covered massive short positions. The move completely reverses the feed cost advantage producers had built up through February. Margin calculations just got turned upside down.

sections.title: GRAINS & OILSEEDS
sections.body: The grain complex got absolutely hammered as managed money (hedge funds) liquidated long positions across the board. Soybeans led the carnage down 3.8% to $11.55 while corn dropped 2.2% to $4.55 — both breaking key technical support levels. Wheat joined the selloff down 1.6% as Black Sea competition intensified and spring planting weather improved. The simultaneous collapse across all three major crops suggests algorithmic selling, not fundamental shifts.
sections.bottom_line: Technical breakdown accelerated fund liquidation across entire grain complex.
sections.farmer_action: Hold pricing decisions — this looks like technical washout, not fundamental shift.

sections.title: LIVESTOCK & DAIRY
sections.body: Livestock futures exploded in the most dramatic reversal in months. Lean hogs surged 8.6% to $93.50 on massive short covering while feeder cattle gained 6.3% as spring demand kicked in early. Live cattle followed with a 3.3% rally as processors competed for supplies. The moves completely flip the feed cost equation — cheaper corn should boost margins, but these price spikes offset most of the benefit.
sections.bottom_line: Explosive livestock rally erases feed cost advantage from grain selloff.
sections.farmer_action: Consider forward pricing summer hogs if you're unhedged — this momentum could continue.

sections.title: ENERGY & INPUTS
sections.body: Energy markets cracked under pressure with WTI crude tumbling 5% below $95 on inventory builds and demand concerns. Natural gas fell 2.2% to $3.02 as spring weather patterns reduced heating demand. The energy collapse should provide some relief on diesel and fertilizer costs heading into planting season. However, the timing couldn't be worse as pre-plant applications are already locked in for most operations.
sections.bottom_line: Energy collapse offers input cost relief but too late for spring applications.
sections.farmer_action: Lock in diesel prices now if you haven't — this energy weakness might not last.

sections.title: MACRO & TRADE
sections.body: The dollar weakened 0.5% to exactly 100.00 on the DXY index as markets absorbed mixed economic signals. The weaker dollar should theoretically support grain exports, but fund liquidation overwhelmed any currency benefit overnight. Stocks rallied 1% as lower commodity prices eased inflation concerns. The divergence between financial markets and agricultural futures highlights how disconnected fund flows have become from farm fundamentals.
sections.bottom_line: Weaker dollar couldn't overcome massive fund liquidation in grains.
sections.farmer_action: Watch export sales data Thursday — weaker dollar should eventually boost demand.

the_more_you_know.title: Why Livestock and Grains Move Together (Until They Don't)
the_more_you_know.body: Historically, livestock and grain futures move inversely — cheaper feed costs boost livestock margins and vice versa. But during extreme technical moves like today, both complexes can divorce from fundamentals entirely. Algorithmic trading amplifies these disconnects, creating temporary opportunities for patient producers. The key is distinguishing between technical washouts and genuine supply/demand shifts.

watch_list.desc: Weekly export sales data — watch for soybean cancellations after overnight collapse
Hog slaughter numbers — need to confirm if supply tightness justified that 8.6% surge
Weekly petroleum inventories — could extend energy sector weakness
Technical levels: Corn support at $4.50, soybeans at $11.40
USDA Prospective Plantings report — the big kahuna for spring planting intentions

daily_quote.text: To own a bit of ground, to scratch it with a hoe, to plant seeds, and watch the renewal of life — this is the commonest delight of the race.

source_summary: CME Group · USDA · Reuters · Yahoo Finance
